issue - closes #17968. What does this implement/fix? This PR adds a new function fwind1 to the scipy.signal module. The fwind1 function can be used to design FIR filters with a 1D windowing approach based on the given specifications of cutoff frequency, passband and stopband characteristics, and window type to create 2D windowing. Any additional information: - Added test cases for the new fwind1 function. - Updated the __all__ attribute in scipy.signal module to include the new function fwind1. please see the following example that generate according to the MATLAB documentation ([link](https://www.mathworks.com/help/images/ref/fwind1.html)) using my implementation. What does this implement/fix? This PR adds a new function fwind1 to the scipy.signal module. issue - This implements issue #17968. What does this implement/fix? This PR adds a new function fwind1 to the scipy.signal module. The fwind1 function can be used to design FIR filters with a 1D windowing approach based on the given specifications of cutoff frequency, passband and stopband characteristics, and window type to create 2D windowing. Any additional information: - Added test cases for the new fwind1 function. - Updated the __all__ attribute in scipy.signal module to include the new function fwind1. please see the following example that generate according to the MATLAB documentation ([link](https://www.mathworks.com/help/images/ref/fwind1.html)) using my implementation. What does this implement/fix? This PR adds a new function fwind1 to the scipy.signal module. The fwind1 function can be used to design FIR filters with a 1D windowing approach based on the given specifications of cutoff frequency, passband and stopband characteristics, and window type to create 2D windowing. Any additional information: - Added test cases for the new fwind1 function. - Updated the __all__ attribute in scipy.signal module to include the new function fwind1. please see the following example that generate according to the MATLAB documentation ([link](https://www.mathworks.com/help/images/ref/fwind1.html)) using my implementation. See + `scipy.signal.get_window` for a list of windows and required + parameters. + fc : float or 1-D array_like + Cutoff frequency of filter (expressed in the same units as `fs`). + fs : float, optional + The sampling frequency of the signal. issue - This implements issue #17968. What does this implement/fix? This PR adds a new function fwind1 to the scipy.signal module. The fwind1 function can be used to design FIR filters with a 1D windowing approach based on the given specifications of cutoff frequency, passband and stopband characteristics, and window type to create 2D windowing. Any additional information: - Added test cases for the new fwind1 function. - Updated the __all__ attribute in scipy.signal module to include the new function fwind1. please see the following example that generate according to the MATLAB documentation ([link](https://www.mathworks.com/help/images/ref/fwind1.html)) using my implementation. issue - This implements issue #17968. What does this implement/fix? This PR adds a new function fwind1 to the scipy.signal module. The fwind1 function can be used to design FIR filters with a 1D windowing approach based on the given specifications of cutoff frequency, passband and stopband characteristics, and window type to create 2D windowing. Any additional information: - Added test cases for the new fwind1 function. - Updated the __all__ attribute in scipy.signal module to include the new function fwind1. please see the following example that generate according to the MATLAB documentation ([link](https://www.mathworks.com/help/images/ref/fwind1.html)) using my implementation. issue - This implements issue #17968. What does this implement/fix? This PR adds a new function fwind1 to the scipy.signal module. The fwind1 function can be used to design FIR filters with a 1D windowing approach based on the given specifications of cutoff frequency, passband and stopband characteristics, and window type to create 2D windowing. Any additional information: - Added test cases for the new fwind1 function. - Updated the __all__ attribute in scipy.signal module to include the new function fwind1. please see the following example that generate according to the MATLAB documentation ([link](https://www.mathworks.com/help/images/ref/fwind1.html)) using my implementation. issue - This implements issue #17968. What does this implement/fix? This PR adds a new function fwind1 to the scipy.signal module. The fwind1 function can be used to design FIR filters with a 1D windowing approach based on the given specifications of cutoff frequency, passband and stopband characteristics, and window type to create 2D windowing. Any additional information: - Added test cases for the new fwind1 function. - Updated the __all__ attribute in scipy.signal module to include the new function fwind1. please see the following example that generate according to the MATLAB documentation ([link](https://www.mathworks.com/help/images/ref/fwind1.html)) using my implementation. issue - This implements issue #17968. What does this implement/fix? This PR adds a new function fwind1 to the scipy.signal module. The fwind1 function can be used to design FIR filters with a 1D windowing approach based on the given specifications of cutoff frequency, passband and stopband characteristics, and window type to create 2D windowing. Any additional information: - Added test cases for the new fwind1 function. - Updated the __all__ attribute in scipy.signal module to include the new function fwind1. please see the following example that generate according to the MATLAB documentation ([link](https://www.mathworks.com/help/images/ref/fwind1.html)) using my implementation. issue - This implements issue #17968. What does this implement/fix? This PR adds a new function fwind1 to the scipy.signal module. The fwind1 function can be used to design FIR filters with a 1D windowing approach based on the given specifications of cutoff frequency, passband and stopband characteristics, and window type to create 2D windowing. Any additional information: - Added test cases for the new fwind1 function. - Updated the __all__ attribute in scipy.signal module to include the new function fwind1. please see the following example that generate according to the MATLAB documentation ([link](https://www.mathworks.com/help/images/ref/fwind1.html)) using my implementation. issue - This implements issue #17968. What does this implement/fix? This PR adds a new function fwind1 to the scipy.signal module. The fwind1 function can be used to design FIR filters with a 1D windowing approach based on the given specifications of cutoff frequency, passband and stopband characteristics, and window type to create 2D windowing. Any additional information: - Added test cases for the new fwind1 function. - Updated the __all__ attribute in scipy.signal module to include the new function fwind1. please see the following example that generate according to the MATLAB documentation ([link](https://www.mathworks.com/help/images/ref/fwind1.html)) using my implementation. issue - This implements issue #17968. What does this implement/fix? This PR adds a new function fwind1 to the scipy.signal module. The fwind1 function can be used to design FIR filters with a 1D windowing approach based on the given specifications of cutoff frequency, passband and stopband characteristics, and window type to create 2D windowing. Any additional information: - Added test cases for the new fwind1 function. - Updated the __all__ attribute in scipy.signal module to include the new function fwind1. please see the following example that generate according to the MATLAB documentation ([link](https://www.mathworks.com/help/images/ref/fwind1.html)) using my implementation. issue - This implements issue #17968. What does this implement/fix? This PR adds a new function fwind1 to the scipy.signal module. The fwind1 function can be used to design FIR filters with a 1D windowing approach based on the given specifications of cutoff frequency, passband and stopband characteristics, and window type to create 2D windowing. Any additional information: - Added test cases for the new fwind1 function. - Updated the __all__ attribute in scipy.signal module to include the new function fwind1. please see the following example that generate according to the MATLAB documentation ([link](https://www.mathworks.com/help/images/ref/fwind1.html)) using my implementation. issue - This implements issue #17968. What does this implement/fix? This PR adds a new function fwind1 to the scipy.signal module. The fwind1 function can be used to design FIR filters with a 1D windowing approach based on the given specifications of cutoff frequency, passband and stopband characteristics, and window type to create 2D windowing. Any additional information: - Added test cases for the new fwind1 function. - Updated the __all__ attribute in scipy.signal module to include the new function fwind1. please see the following example that generate according to the MATLAB documentation ([link](https://www.mathworks.com/help/images/ref/fwind1.html)) using my implementation. The filter is separable with linear phase; it will be designed + as a product of two 1D filters with dimensions defined by `hsize`. + + Parameters + ---------- + hsize : tuple or list of length 2 + Lengths of the filter in each dimension. `hsize[0]` specifies the + number of coefficients in the row direction and `hsize[1]` specifies + the number of coefficients in the column direction. + window : tuple or list of length 2 + Desired window to use for each 1D filter. Each element should be + a string or tuple of string and parameter values. See + `scipy.signal.get_window` for a list of windows and required + parameters. + fc : float or 1-D array_like + Cutoff frequency of filter (expressed in the same units as `fs`). + fs : float, optional + The sampling frequency of the signal. Default is 2. + + Returns + ------- + filter_2d : (hsize[0], hsize[1]) ndarray + Coefficients of 2D FIR filter. + + Raises + ------ + ValueError + If `hsize` and `window` are not 2-element tuples or lists. + + See Also + -------- + scipy.signal.firwin + + Examples + -------- + Generate a 5x5 low-pass filter with cutoff frequency 0.1. + + >>> import numpy as np + >>> from scipy.signal import get_window + >>> filter_2d = fwind1((5, 5), (get_window(('kaiser', 5.0)), 'boxcar'), 0.1) + >>> filter_2d + array([[0.003, 0.023, 0.052, 0.023, 0.003], + [0.023, 0.173, 0.391, 0.173, 0.023], + [0.052, 0.391, 0.882, 0.391, 0.052], + [0.023, 0.173, 0.391, 0.173, 0.023], + [0.003, 0.023, 0.052, 0.023, 0.003]]) + """ + if len(hsize) != 2: + raise ValueError("hsize must be a 2-element tuple or list") + + if len(window) != 2: + raise ValueError("window must be a 2-element tuple or list") + + row_filter = firwin(hsize[0], cutoff=fc, window=window[0], fs=fs) + col_filter = firwin(hsize[1], cutoff=fc, window=window[1], fs=fs) + + row_filter_2d = row_filter.reshape(-1, 1) + col_filter_2d = col_filter.reshape(1, -1) + + filter_2d = row_filter_2d @ col_filter_2d + + return issue - This implements issue #17968. What does this implement/fix? This PR adds a new function fwind1 to the scipy.signal module. The fwind1 function can be used to design FIR filters with a 1D windowing approach based on the given specifications of cutoff frequency, passband and stopband characteristics, and window type to create 2D windowing. Any additional information: - Added test cases for the new fwind1 function. - Updated the __all__ attribute in scipy.signal module to include the new function fwind1. please see the following example that generate according to the MATLAB documentation ([link](https://www.mathworks.com/help/images/ref/fwind1.html)) using my implementation. issue - This implements issue #17968. What does this implement/fix? This PR adds a new function fwind1 to the scipy.signal module. The fwind1 function can be used to design FIR filters with a 1D windowing approach based on the given specifications of cutoff frequency, passband and stopband characteristics, and window type to create 2D windowing. Any additional information: - Added test cases for the new fwind1 function. - Updated the __all__ attribute in scipy.signal module to include the new function fwind1. please see the following example that generate according to the MATLAB documentation ([link](https://www.mathworks.com/help/images/ref/fwind1.html)) using my implementation. The filter is separable with linear phase; it will be designed + as a product of two 1D filters with dimensions defined by `hsize`. + + Parameters + ---------- + hsize : tuple or list of length 2 + Lengths of the filter in each dimension. `hsize[0]` specifies the + number of coefficients in the row direction and `hsize[1]` specifies + the number of coefficients in the column direction. + window : tuple or list of length 2 + Desired window to use for each 1D filter. Each element should be + a string or tuple of string and parameter values. See + `scipy.signal.get_window` for a list of windows and required + parameters. + fc : float or 1-D array_like + Cutoff frequency of filter (expressed in the same units as `fs`). + fs : float, optional + The sampling frequency of the signal. Default is 2. + + Returns + ------- + filter_2d : (hsize[0], hsize[1]) ndarray + Coefficients of 2D FIR filter. + + Raises + ------ + ValueError + If `hsize` and `window` are not 2-element tuples or lists. + + See Also + -------- + scipy.signal.firwin + + Examples + -------- + Generate a 5x5 low-pass filter with cutoff frequency 0.1. + + >>> import numpy as np + >>> from scipy.signal import get_window + >>> filter_2d = fwind1((5, 5), (get_window(('kaiser', 5.0)), 'boxcar'), 0.1) + >>> filter_2d + array([[0.003, 0.023, 0.052, 0.023, 0.003], + [0.023, 0.173, 0.391, 0.173, 0.023], + [0.052, 0.391, 0.882, 0.391, 0.052], + [0.023, 0.173, 0.391, 0.173, 0.023], + [0.003, 0.023, 0.052, 0.023, 0.003]]) + """ + if len(hsize) != 2: + raise ValueError("hsize must be a 2-element tuple or list") + + if len(window) != 2: + raise ValueError("window must be a 2-element tuple or list") + + row_filter = firwin(hsize[0], cutoff=fc, window=window[0], fs=fs) + col_filter = firwin(hsize[1], cutoff=fc, window=window[1], fs=fs) + + return np.outer(row_filter, issue - This implements issue #17968. What does this implement/fix? This PR adds a new function fwind1 to the scipy.signal module. The fwind1 function can be used to design FIR filters with a 1D windowing approach based on the given specifications of cutoff frequency, passband and stopband characteristics, and window type to create 2D windowing. Any additional information: - Added test cases for the new fwind1 function. - Updated the __all__ attribute in scipy.signal module to include the new function fwind1. please see the following example that generate according to the MATLAB documentation ([link](https://www.mathworks.com/help/images/ref/fwind1.html)) using my implementation. issue - This implements issue #17968. What does this implement/fix? This PR adds a new function fwind1 to the scipy.signal module. The fwind1 function can be used to design FIR filters with a 1D windowing approach based on the given specifications of cutoff frequency, passband and stopband characteristics, and window type to create 2D windowing. Any additional information: - Added test cases for the new fwind1 function. - Updated the __all__ attribute in scipy.signal module to include the new function fwind1. please see the following example that generate according to the MATLAB documentation ([link](https://www.mathworks.com/help/images/ref/fwind1.html)) using my implementation. What does this implement/fix? This PR adds a new function fwind1 to the scipy.signal module. The fwind1 function can be used to design FIR filters with a 1D windowing approach based on the given specifications of cutoff frequency, passband and stopband characteristics, and window type to create 2D windowing. Any additional information: - Added test cases for the new fwind1 function. - Updated the __all__ attribute in scipy.signal module to include the new function fwind1. - Apply Black formatter to the relevant files please see the following example that generate according to the MATLAB documentation ([link](https://www.mathworks.com/help/images/ref/fwind1.html)) using my implementation.
